subject: PhD Algebra
instructions: Answer seven problems. Write your answers clearly in complete English sentences. You may quote results (within reason) as long as you state them clearly.

problems.text: Let \(K\) be a finite field, and let \(F\) be a subfield of \(K\). Prove that the extension \(K/F\) is Galois.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Determine a splitting field \(K\) for the polynomial \(X^3-2\in\mathbb{Q}[X]\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Determine \(\operatorname{Gal}(K/\mathbb{Q})\) and give an explicit description of the action of \(\operatorname{Gal}(K/\mathbb{Q})\) on \(K\).

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: For each subgroup \(H\) of \(\operatorname{Gal}(K/\mathbb{Q})\) find the subfield of \(K\) fixed by \(H\).

problems.text: Let \(A\) and \(B\) be abelian groups, and \(T=A\otimes_{\mathbb{Z}}B\). Let \(\phi:A\to A\) be a group homomorphism. Prove that there is a group homomorphism \(\psi:T\to T\) such that, for all \(a\in A\) and \(b\in B\), we have \(\psi(a\otimes_{\mathbb{Z}}b)=\phi(a)\otimes_{\mathbb{Z}}b\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Let \(\mathcal{C}\) be a concrete category and let \(S\) be a set. Define what it means to say that an object in \(\mathcal{C}\) is free for \(S\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that any two objects in \(\mathcal{C}\) which are free for \(S\) are equivalent in \(\mathcal{C}\).

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Define what it means for an \(R\)-module \(M\) to be projective.

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Prove that any free \(R\)-module is projective.

problems.text: Let \(F\) be a free group on a set \(S\). Prove that \(F\) is abelian if and only if \(|S|\leq 1\).
State and prove Hilbert’s Basis Theorem.

Let \(R\) be a commutative ring with identity. Let \(S\) be a non-empty subset of \(R\) which is closed under multiplication and is such that \(0\notin S\). Prove that there exists a prime ideal \(P\) of \(R\) such that \(P\cap S=\varnothing\).
Let \(R\) be a Dedekind domain, and let \(I\) be a non-zero ideal of \(R\). Prove that \(R/I\) is Artinian.

subparts.label: (a)
subparts.text: Let \(R\) be an integral domain. Define what is a fractional ideal of \(R\).

subparts.label: (b)
subparts.text: Define what it means for a fractional ideal of \(R\) to be invertible.

subparts.label: (c)
subparts.text: Prove from your definition that if \(R\) is a principal ideal domain then every fractional ideal of \(R\) is invertible.

problems.text: Let \(R\) and \(S\) be non-commutative semisimple rings with \(|R|=|S|=2^2\cdot 3^4\cdot 5\). Does it follow that \(R\) is isomorphic to \(S\)? Justify your answer.
